- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

56	===== 2a. CONTRACT DATA MODEL — deontic-strength enum + generic-IR vocabulary (planning_gate_schema.py) =====
57	
58	# NOTE ON NAMING: the plan says "deliverable_spec / scope_spec". In the ACTUAL baseline the
59	# ResearchContract holds these as FLAT `list[ContractTerm]` keyed by a free-string `dimension`
60	# (e.g. "scope.source_types", "deliverable.kind"). There is no separate DeliverableSpec class.
61	# The "deontic-strength enum" is the FORCE_* constants below (hard | preference | open); a term's
62	# strength lives in `ContractTerm.force`, and a DeonticCue (clause_ledger.py) carries the cue's force.
63	
64	# --- FORCE (the deontic-strength enum) — planning_gate_schema.py:94 ---
65	FORCE_HARD = "hard"            # binding inclusion / exclusion / shape requirement
66	FORCE_PREFER = "preference"    # optimize when feasible; may NEVER starve coverage
67	FORCE_OPEN = "open"            # intentionally unspecified
68	FORCES: frozenset[str] = frozenset({FORCE_HARD, FORCE_PREFER, FORCE_OPEN})
69	
70	STAGES: frozenset[str] = frozenset({"retrieval","outline","compose","render","audit"})
71	
72	# --- generic relation a term asserts (Phase B constraint-IR) — :121 ---
73	OP_IN = "IN"            # membership: attribute ∈ value_set   (allowed-kind set)
74	OP_NOT_IN = "NOT_IN"    # exclusion:  attribute ∉ value_set   ("no blogs")
75	OP_EQ = "EQ"            # equality:   attribute == value
76	OP_GTE = "GTE"          # lower bound: attribute >= value      (from 2024 onward)
77	OP_LTE = "LTE"          # upper bound: attribute <= value      (before 2020)
78	OP_BETWEEN = "BETWEEN"  # interval:   value <= attribute <= value2
79	OP_REQUIRE = "REQUIRE"  # obligation: attribute must be covered/present
80	OP_PREFER = "PREFER"    # soft optimize: attribute preferred, never gates
81	OPERATORS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({OP_IN,OP_NOT_IN,OP_EQ,OP_GTE,OP_LTE,OP_BETWEEN,OP_REQUIRE,OP_PREFER})
82	
83	STAGE_OWNERS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({"retrieval","ranking","eligibility","compose","render"})
84	
85	# normalization status: exact (registry-mapped) | proposed (LLM, unconfirmed) | opaque (preserved
86	# verbatim, cannot yet enforce). An opaque HARD term is lossless-preserved + surfaced as
87	# blocked_unsupported — never silently dropped. — :149
88	NORM_EXACT = "exact"; NORM_PROPOSED = "proposed"; NORM_OPAQUE = "opaque"
89	
90	# --- canonical SOURCE-scope dimension keys (what the retrieval projection reads) — :168 ---
91	SCOPE_SOURCE_TYPES = "scope.source_types"          # e.g. journal_article
92	SCOPE_SOURCE_QUALITY = "scope.source_quality"      # e.g. high (high-quality/peer-reviewed)
93	SCOPE_SOURCE_LANGUAGES = "scope.source_languages"  # SOURCE language, e.g. en / English
94	SCOPE_EXCLUDED_SOURCE_KINDS = "scope.excluded_source_kinds"  # "no blogs" -> op=exclude facet, NEVER query text
95	SCOPE_OPAQUE = "scope.opaque"       # deontic clause no parser normalized; post-fetch eligibility judged
96	CONTENT_OPAQUE = "content.opaque"   # topic obligation; a compose-side note
97	SCHEMA_VERSION = "planning-gate/1.0"
98	
99	# --- ContractTerm (the tagged unit; strength = .force) — :391 ---
100	@dataclass
101	class ContractTerm:
102	    term_id: str
103	    dimension: str
104	    value: Any = None
105	    origin: str = ORIGIN_INFERRED
106	    force: str = FORCE_OPEN            # <-- deontic strength: hard | preference | open
107	    confidence: float = 0.0
108	    spans: list[PromptSpan] = field(default_factory=list)
109	    rationale: str = ""
110	    policy_id: Optional[str] = None
111	    enforcement_stages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
112	    editable: bool = True
113	    # generic constraint-IR fields (Phase B; all OPTIONAL / default-empty; to_dict skips at default
114	    # so an old term hashes byte-identically — the OFF-path guardrail):
115	    subject: str = ""                    # e.g. source, evidence, output, section
116	    attribute: str = ""                  # e.g. kind, published_at, language, quality
117	    operator: str = ""                   # one of OPERATORS ("" == unset / legacy)
118	    value_set: list[Any] = field(default_factory=list)  # for IN / NOT_IN
119	    boolean_group: str = ""

121	    capability_id: str = ""
122	    normalization_status: str = ""       # exact | proposed | opaque
123	
124	    def is_hard(self) -> bool: return self.force == FORCE_HARD
125	    def is_opaque(self) -> bool: return self.normalization_status == NORM_OPAQUE
126	
127	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
128	        out = {"term_id":self.term_id,"dimension":self.dimension,"value":self.value,
129	               "origin":self.origin,"force":self.force,"confidence":self.confidence,

131	               "policy_id":self.policy_id,"enforcement_stages":list(self.enforcement_stages),
132	               "editable":self.editable}
133	        # emit each generic-IR field ONLY when set (non-default) -> legacy shape hashes byte-identical
134	        if self.subject: out["subject"] = self.subject
135	        if self.attribute: out["attribute"] = self.attribute
136	        if self.operator: out["operator"] = self.operator
137	        if self.value_set: out["value_set"] = list(self.value_set)

140	        if self.capability_id: out["capability_id"] = self.capability_id

143	    # from_dict: reads generic-IR fields if present; an out-of-vocab operator/stage_owner is kept

146	===== 2b. ResearchContract (the typed contract; scope/deliverable are ContractTerm lists) — planning_gate_schema.py:746 =====

148	@dataclass
149	class ResearchContract:
150	    """Grouped as objective / scope / content / deliverable, held as flat ContractTerm lists
151	    keyed by `dimension` (objective.question, scope.source_types, scope.date, deliverable.kind)
152	    plus the two structured collections (coverage, sections)."""
153	    schema_version: str = SCHEMA_VERSION
154	    objective: list[ContractTerm] = field(default_factory=list)
155	    scope: list[ContractTerm] = field(default_factory=list)
156	    content_terms: list[ContractTerm] = field(default_factory=list)

169	        terms.extend(self.content_terms); terms.extend(self.deliverable)

182	                "scope":[t.to_dict() for t in self.scope],

192	===== 2c. RetrievalPolicy — allowed_source_kinds + the contract->enforcer projection (retrieval_projection.py:185, :314) =====

194	@dataclass
195	class RetrievalPolicy:
196	    """Typed, hash-stamped policy compiled from the pinned contract + plan. The DECLARATIVE
197	    predicate set a caller executes as backend filters, prefetch ranking, and a post-fetch
198	    citable-eligibility verdict — ALWAYS upstream of the frozen verifier (changes which rows are
199	    ELIGIBLE to cite, never HOW a claim is verified). Pure data: no network, no LLM, no I/O."""
200	    allowed_source_kinds: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # hard scope.source_types values; kind matching NONE (when non-empty) is out of the citable menu
201	    excluded_source_kinds: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # NEGATIVE predicate; matching ANY is masked; NEVER positive query text
202	    date_from: Optional[str] = None
203	    date_to: Optional[str] = None
204	    languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)              # hard scope.source_languages
205	    named_inclusions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
206	    named_exclusions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
207	    quality_profile: Optional[str] = None                          # REF to a domain-neutral quality profile (e.g. "high"); never a query word
208	    opaque_eligibility: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)     # F2: hard un-normalized deontic clauses, judged post-fetch; NEVER a query string
209	    predicate_force: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)   # {predicate_key: "hard"|"soft"} — which are gating vs ranking-only
210	    contract_hash: str = ""
211	
212	    def is_empty(self) -> bool:  # permissive/empty-contract case => caller applies NO filter (byte-identical)
213	        return not (self.allowed_source_kinds or self.excluded_source_kinds or self.date_from
214	                    or self.date_to or self.languages or self.named_inclusions
215	                    or self.named_exclusions or self.quality_profile or self.opaque_eligibility)
216	    def to_dict(self): ...  # straight field dump  [elided]
217	
218	    # -- the contract -> protocol BRIDGE: compiles into the LEGACY `protocol` shape the FROZEN
219	    #    constraint_enforcement.build_scope_enforcement reads. allowed_source_kinds -> op=prefer
220	    #    facets; excluded_source_kinds -> op=exclude facets. Strictness=hard iff predicate marked hard.
221	    #    A value resolving to no facet is emitted as opaque:<value> (NEVER silently dropped).
222	    def to_scope_protocol(self, *, ontology=None) -> dict[str, Any]:
223	        facets: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
224	        seen_facets: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()
225	        def _facet(facet_id, dimension, op, hard):
226	            key = (facet_id, op)
227	            if not facet_id or key in seen_facets: return

302	def score_source_quality(row) -> tuple[str, float, str]:
303	    """Returns (verdict in {PASS,FAIL,UNKNOWN}, weight in (0,1], basis).
304	    PASS = positively-evidenced high quality; FAIL = positively-evidenced low quality
305	    (retracted / predatory host / low tier / non-peer-reviewed shell); UNKNOWN = no usable quality
306	    metadata (fail-CLOSED at the hard seam). weight = the SOFT-preference demote (never 0)."""
307	    # 1) retraction — always disqualifying, highest precedence.
308	    if bool(_row_get(row, "is_retracted", False)):
309	        return FAIL, 0.05, "retracted (is_retracted=True)"
310	    url = _row_url(row)
311	    # 2) predatory / OJS-mill host — journal-shaped but not peer-reviewed.
312	    if _predatory_host(url):
313	        return FAIL, 0.1, f"predatory/OJS-mill host heuristic matched ({url})"
314	    tier = str(_row_get(row, "tier", "") or "").strip().upper()
315	    pr = _row_get(row, "is_peer_reviewed", None)  # None = not fetched (absent)
316	    # 3) explicit peer-review evidence.

318	        return PASS, 1.0, f"peer_reviewed=True AND tier={tier} (high-quality band)"
319	    if pr is False:   # journal-SHAPED but explicitly NOT peer-reviewed -> FAIL  (:209)
320	        return FAIL, 0.2, f"is_peer_reviewed=False (journal-shaped != peer-reviewed); tier={tier or 'UNKNOWN'}"
321	    # 4) tier-only evidence (peer-review flag absent).
322	    if tier in _HIGH_QUALITY_TIERS:
323	        return PASS, 1.0, f"tier={tier} (high-quality band; peer_reviewed flag absent)"
324	    if tier in _LOW_QUALITY_TIERS:
325	        return FAIL, 0.3, f"tier={tier} (low-quality band: industry/news/blog/stub)"
326	    # 5) content-shell degradation -> UNKNOWN (fail-closed under hard) with a demote.  (:222)
327	    if (bool(_row_get(row,"content_starved",False)) or bool(_row_get(row,"landing_page",False))

330	    # 6) no usable quality metadata -> UNKNOWN (fail-CLOSED under hard; soft keeps neutral weight). (:226)
331	    return UNKNOWN, 0.6, f"no quality metadata (tier={tier or 'UNKNOWN'}, peer_reviewed absent) — fail-closed under hard"

340	    force = (getattr(policy,"predicate_force",{}) or {}).get("quality_profile","soft")
341	    is_hard = str(force).strip().lower() == "hard"

343	    for row in list(evidence_rows or []):

346	        verdict, weight, basis = score_source_quality(row)
347	        plan.receipts.append(SourceReceipt(contract_hash=contract_hash, term_id=quality_term_id,
348	            source_id=url, stage=STAGE_QUALITY, verdict=verdict, basis=basis))

361	#     (see the `else: plan.url_to_quality_weight[url] = max(0.05, score)` at :351). — :289 ---
362	def build_topicality_eligibility(evidence_rows, *, objective, thread_queries=None, floor,
363	        scorer, contract_hash="", topicality_term_id="topicality", is_hard=True) -> EligibilityPlan:
364	    """Post-fetch topical eligibility vs the CLEAN objective + owning thread. Reuses the SAME
365	    embedding-cosine scorer live retrieval uses pre-fetch. Confirmed off-topic (score < floor) is
366	    quarantined (HARD) / down-ranked (SOFT); uncertain (>= floor) stays. None score => UNKNOWN,
367	    fail-OPEN (never quarantine a source we could not score — topicality is not a user prohibition)."""
368	    plan = EligibilityPlan()
369	    rows = list(evidence_rows or [])
370	    if not rows or not (objective or "").strip(): return plan
371	    row_dicts = [{"statement": _row_text(r), "direct_quote": ""} for r in rows]
372	    try: score_map = scorer(objective, list(thread_queries or []), row_dicts)
373	    except Exception: score_map = None
374	    if score_map is None:
375	        # embedder unavailable -> UNKNOWN, fail-OPEN (no quarantine); receipts only.  [elided loop]
376	        return plan
377	    for idx, r in enumerate(rows):
378	        url = _row_url(r)
379	        if not url: continue
380	        score = float(score_map.get(idx, 0.0))
381	        if score < floor:
382	            verdict = FAIL
383	            basis = f"topicality cosine {score:.3f} < floor {floor:.3f} (confirmed off-topic)"
384	            if is_hard:
385	                plan.eligibility_excluded_ids.add(url)
386	                plan.excluded_records.append({"source_id":url,"stage":STAGE_TOPICALITY,
387	                    "verdict":verdict,"basis":basis,"force":"hard"})
388	            else:
389	                plan.url_to_quality_weight[url] = max(0.05, score)   # <-- :351 SOFT-demote (Fix 5c band)
390	        else:
391	            verdict = PASS; basis = f"topicality cosine {score:.3f} >= floor {floor:.3f}"
392	        plan.receipts.append(SourceReceipt(contract_hash=contract_hash, term_id=topicality_term_id,
393	            source_id=url, stage=STAGE_TOPICALITY, verdict=verdict, basis=basis))
394	    return plan

229	### B.(b) General credibility-tier model for UNKNOWN resolution
230	
231	Today `score_source_quality` (`quality_eligibility.py:177/:302`) falls through to UNKNOWN
232	(fail-closed under hard) at `:222/:226` whenever tier metadata is absent — 41% of the corpus.
233	Fix 5b's rescue predicate (`is_peer_reviewed_journal_article` / DOI) is journal-literal. General
234	model — a static, deterministic **signal→tier table** over fields the pipeline already fetches
235	(no new subsystem; ~30 LOC in `quality_eligibility.py`):
236	
237	```python
238	# Tiers: T1 = authoritative-universal; T2 = reputable-secondary; T3 = interested-primary.
239	# (T4/low already exists as _LOW_QUALITY_TIERS FAIL band — untouched.)
240	def _positive_signal_tier(row) -> tuple[str, str] | None:   # -> (tier, kind) or None
241	    if _row_get(row, "doi") or is_peer_reviewed_journal_article(row):
242	        return ("T1", "peer_reviewed_journal")          # scholarly: ONE T1 instance
243	    if _official_gov_host(row):                          # facet-classifier gov facet / registry TLD
244	        return ("T1", "government")                      # official/primary: ANOTHER T1 instance
245	    if _reputable_newsroom(row):                         # wire/major-outlet facet, tier not low
246	        return ("T2", "news")
247	    if _issuer_primary(row):                             # press release / corp blog on issuer domain
248	        return ("T3", "press_release" if _is_pr(row) else "blog")
249	    return None
250	
251	# Resolution rule (inserted in score_source_quality AFTER step 4, BEFORE the :222 shell check):
252	#   T1                                  -> PASS   (universal: quality is quality on any contract)
253	#   T2/T3 and kind in allowed_kinds     -> PASS   (contract explicitly wants this kind)
254	#   kind in excluded_kinds              -> never PASS here (fall through; exclusion facet masks anyway)
255	#   otherwise                           -> fall through to existing UNKNOWN + demote weight (DEMOTE)
256	```

311	def corpus_kind_adequacy(rows, allowed_kinds, *, min_rows=25) -> tuple[bool, int]:

313	            if classified_kind(r) in allowed_kinds
314	            and score_source_quality(r, allowed_kinds=allowed_kinds)[0] != FAIL)

=== score_source_quality full body ===
def score_source_quality(row: Any) -> tuple[str, float, str]:
    """Deterministic, domain-neutral quality verdict for one fetched row.

    Returns ``(verdict, weight, basis)``:
      * ``verdict`` in {PASS, FAIL, UNKNOWN} for a HARD "only high-quality" predicate.
        PASS = positively-evidenced high quality; FAIL = positively-evidenced low
        quality (retracted / predatory host / low tier / non-peer-reviewed shell);
        UNKNOWN = no usable quality metadata (fail-CLOSED at the hard seam).
      * ``weight`` in (0, 1] — the SOFT-preference demote (never 0; order-not-drop).
      * ``basis`` — the rule + evidence that fired (for the receipt).

    "journal-shaped" is NOT peer-reviewed: an ``is_peer_reviewed`` flag that is
    explicitly False (fetched, not merely absent) is a FAIL even if the venue looks
    journal-like. A retraction is always a FAIL. Absent metadata is UNKNOWN.
    """
    # 1) retraction — always disqualifying, highest precedence.
    if bool(_row_get(row, "is_retracted", False)):
        return FAIL, 0.05, "retracted (is_retracted=True)"

    url = _row_url(row)
    # 2) predatory / OJS-mill host — journal-shaped but not peer-reviewed.
    if _predatory_host(url):
        return FAIL, 0.1, f"predatory/OJS-mill host heuristic matched ({url})"

    tier = str(_row_get(row, "tier", "") or "").strip().upper()
    pr = _row_get(row, "is_peer_reviewed", None)  # None = not fetched (absent)

    # 3) explicit peer-review evidence.
    if pr is True and tier in _HIGH_QUALITY_TIERS:
        return PASS, 1.0, f"peer_reviewed=True AND tier={tier} (high-quality band)"
    if pr is False:
        # journal-SHAPED but explicitly NOT peer-reviewed -> not high quality.
        return FAIL, 0.2, f"is_peer_reviewed=False (journal-shaped != peer-reviewed); tier={tier or 'UNKNOWN'}"

    # 4) tier-only evidence (peer-review flag absent).
    if tier in _HIGH_QUALITY_TIERS:
        return PASS, 1.0, f"tier={tier} (high-quality band; peer_reviewed flag absent)"
    if tier in _LOW_QUALITY_TIERS:
        return FAIL, 0.3, f"tier={tier} (low-quality band: industry/news/blog/stub)"

    # 5) content-shell degradation is a quality-of-content negative but NOT a hard
    #    quality FAIL on its own — it is UNKNOWN (fail-closed under hard) with a demote.
    if (bool(_row_get(row, "content_starved", False))
            or bool(_row_get(row, "landing_page", False))
            or bool(_row_get(row, "citation_metadata_shell", False))):
        return UNKNOWN, 0.4, "content shell/starved (no quality metadata; fail-closed under hard)"

    # 6) no usable quality metadata at all -> UNKNOWN (fail-CLOSED under a hard
    #    "only high-quality"; a soft preference keeps it at neutral weight).
    return UNKNOWN, 0.6, f"no quality metadata (tier={tier or 'UNKNOWN'}, peer_reviewed absent) — fail-closed under hard"
